infobox.value: * Airport Road * [Big Trails](/wiki/Big_Trails,_Wyoming "Big Trails, Wyoming") * [Mc Nutt](/wiki/Mc_Nutt,_Wyoming "Mc Nutt, Wyoming") * [South Flat](/wiki/South_Flat,_Wyoming "South Flat, Wyoming") * [Washakie Ten](/wiki/Washakie_Ten,_Wyoming "Washakie Ten, Wyoming") * [West River](/wiki/West_River,_Wyoming "West River, Wyoming") * [Winchester](/wiki/Winchester,_Wyoming "Winchester, Wyoming")

is an [unincorporated community](/wiki/Unincorporated_area "Unincorporated area") in [Washakie County](/wiki/Washakie_County,_Wyoming "Washakie County, Wyoming"), [Wyoming](/wiki/Wyoming "Wyoming"), United States. The population was 297 at the [2000 census](/wiki/United_States_Census_2000 "United States Census 2000"), when it was a [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place") (CDP).

**Airport Road** is an [unincorporated community](/wiki/Unincorporated_area "Unincorporated area") in [Washakie County](/wiki/Washakie_County,_Wyoming "Washakie County, Wyoming"), [Wyoming](/wiki/Wyoming "Wyoming"), United States. The population was 297 at the [2000 census](/wiki/United_States_Census_2000 "United States Census 2000"), when it was a [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place") (CDP).

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Airport_Road,_Wyoming&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), in 2000 the CDP has a total area of 3.7 square miles (9.7 km2), all land.

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Airport_Road,_Wyoming&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Demographics")]

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census")[[1]](#cite_note-GR2-1) of 2000, there were 297 people, 110 households, and 85 families residing in the CDP. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 79.6 people per square mile (30.7/km2). There were 114 housing units at an average density of 30.6/sq mi (11.8/km2). The racial makeup of the CDP was 89.56% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 0.34% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 7.07% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 3.03% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 12.79% of the population.

There were 110 households, out of which 33.6% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 71.8% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 1.8% had a female householder with no husband present, and 22.7% were non-families. 20.0% of all households were made up of individuals, and 8.2% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.70 and the average family size was 3.15.

In the CDP the population was spread out, with 26.3% under the age of 18, 6.7% from 18 to 24, 21.2% from 25 to 44, 29.0% from 45 to 64, and 16.8% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 43 years. For every 100 females, there were 91.6 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 99.1 males.

The median income for a household in the CDP was $66,806, and the median income for a family was $80,221. Males had a median income of $31,042 versus $20,458 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the CDP was $27,891. None of the population or families were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line").

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Airport_Road,_Wyoming&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Education")]

Public education in the community of Airport Road is provided by [Washakie County School District #1](/wiki/Washakie_County_School_District_Number_1 "Washakie County School District Number 1"). The district operates five campuses – East Side Elementary, South Side Elementary, West Side Elementary, Worland Middle School, and [Worland High School](/wiki/Worland_High_School "Worland High School") [[1]](https://web.archive.org/web/20090907143528/http://www.worlandhs.com/).
